item.title: Noncollinear ferromagnetism in the Kondo-lattice compound ${\mathrm{Ce}}_{5}{\mathrm{CoGe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Jinyu Wu, Jiawen Zhang, Toni Shiroka, Shams Sohel Islam, Mingyi Wang, Yongjun Zhang, Devashibhai T. Adroja, Yu Liu, Huiqiu Yuan, and Michael Smidman

The dense Kondo lattice ${\mathrm{Ce}}_{5}{\mathrm{CoGe}}_{2}$ exhibits superconductivity after the magnetic ordering is suppressed by pressure. Here the ambient pressure magnetic state is investigated via magnetization, heat capacity, powder neutron diffraction, and muon-spin relaxation measurement…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134431] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jinyu Wu, Jiawen Zhang, Toni Shiroka, Shams Sohel Islam, Mingyi Wang, Yongjun Zhang, Devashibhai T. Adroja, Yu Liu, Huiqiu Yuan, and Michael Smidman

The dense Kondo lattice Ce5CoGe2 exhibits superconductivity after the magnetic ordering is suppressed by pressure. Here the ambient pressure magnetic state is investigated via magnetization, heat capacity, powder neutron diffraction, and muon-spin relaxation measurements. Neutron diffraction results…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134431] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Noncollinear ferromagnetism in the Kondo-lattice compound ${\mathrm{Ce}}_{5}{\mathrm{CoGe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Jinyu Wu, Jiawen Zhang, Toni Shiroka, Shams Sohel Islam, Mingyi Wang, Yongjun Zhang, Devashibhai T. Adroja, Yu Liu, Huiqiu Yuan, and Michael Smidman
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Hidden magnon Berry curvature drives vertical magnon transport
item.description:
Author(s): Atul Rathor, Sahanawaj Akhtar, and Arijit Haldar

The authors show here that quasi-two-dimensional magnetic insulators comprising a few layers are fundamentally distinct, at the quantum geometric level, from perfectly flat single-layered 2D magnets. They discover that magnon excitations in such quasi-2D insulators experience in-plane components of a bosonic symplectic Berry curvature, absent in flat 2D magnetic insulators. They call this the “hidden magnon Berry curvature” (HMBC). They propose that HMBC can generate (and be detected via) magnon transport perpendicular to the layers of van der Waals materials in response to thermal or magnetic-field gradients. Their work points toward the possibility of yet-undiscovered hidden quantum geometric transport in layered quasi-2D magnets.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144426] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Atul Rathor, Sahanawaj Akhtar, and Arijit Haldar

The authors show here that quasi-two-dimensional magnetic insulators comprising a few layers are fundamentally distinct, at the quantum geometric level, from perfectly flat single-layered 2D magnets. They discover that magnon excitations in such quasi-2D insulators experience in-plane components of a bosonic symplectic Berry curvature, absent in flat 2D magnetic insulators. They call this the “hidden magnon Berry curvature” (HMBC). They propose that HMBC can generate (and be detected via) magnon transport perpendicular to the layers of van der Waals materials in response to thermal or magnetic-field gradients. Their work points toward the possibility of yet-undiscovered hidden quantum geometric transport in layered quasi-2D magnets.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144426] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Hidden magnon Berry curvature drives vertical magnon transport
item.dc:creator: Atul Rathor, Sahanawaj Akhtar, and Arijit Haldar
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Pathways from a chiral superconductor to a composite Fermi liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Yunchao Zhang, Leyna Shackleton, and T. Senthil

Recent experiments have reported chiral time-reversal broken superconductivity in $n$-layer rhombohedral graphene for $n=4,\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}5,\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\text{and}\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}6$. Introducing a moiré potential by alignment with a hexagonal boron nitride sub…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144510] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yunchao Zhang, Leyna Shackleton, and T. Senthil

Recent experiments have reported chiral time-reversal broken superconductivity in n-layer rhombohedral graphene for n=4,5,and6. Introducing a moiré potential by alignment with a hexagonal boron nitride substrate suppresses the superconductivity but leads instead to various fractional quantum anomalo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144510] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Pathways from a chiral superconductor to a composite Fermi liquid
item.dc:creator: Yunchao Zhang, Leyna Shackleton, and T. Senthil
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Construction of asymptotic quantum many-body scar states in the $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Daiki Hashimoto, Masaya Kunimi, and Tetsuro Nikuni

We construct asymptotic quantum many-body scars (AQMBS) in one-dimensional $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Hubbard chains ($N≥3$) by embedding the scar subspace into an auxiliary Hilbert subspace ${\mathcal{H}}_{P}$ and identifying a parent Hamiltonian within it, together with a corresponding extension of the rest…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155137] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Daiki Hashimoto, Masaya Kunimi, and Tetsuro Nikuni

We construct asymptotic quantum many-body scars (AQMBS) in one-dimensional SU(N) Hubbard chains (N≥3) by embedding the scar subspace into an auxiliary Hilbert subspace HP and identifying a parent Hamiltonian within it, together with a corresponding extension of the restricted spectrum-generating alg…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155137] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Construction of asymptotic quantum many-body scar states in the $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Hubbard model
item.dc:creator: Daiki Hashimoto, Masaya Kunimi, and Tetsuro Nikuni

item.title: Symmetry-directed electronic and optical properties in a two-dimensional square-lattice ZnPc metal-organic framework
item.description:
Author(s): Zhonghui Han, Lanting Feng, Guodong Yu, and Shengjun Yuan

The electronic structure of materials is fundamentally governed by their crystal symmetry. While most research on two-dimensional materials has focused on hexagonal lattices, such as graphene, hexagonal boron nitride, and transition metal dichalcogenides. This work explores a square-lattice system: …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165417] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhonghui Han, Lanting Feng, Guodong Yu, and Shengjun Yuan

The electronic structure of materials is fundamentally governed by their crystal symmetry. While most research on two-dimensional materials has focused on hexagonal lattices, such as graphene, hexagonal boron nitride, and transition metal dichalcogenides. This work explores a square-lattice system: …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165417] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026

item.title: Layer Hall effect induced by altermagnetism
item.description:
Author(s): Fang Qin (覃昉) and Rui Chen

In this work, we propose a scheme to realize the layer Hall effect in the ferromagnetic topological insulator ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Se}}_{3}$ via proximity to $d$-wave altermagnets. We show that an altermagnet and an in-plane magnetic field applied near one surface gap the corresponding Dirac c…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165418] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fang Qin (覃昉) and Rui Chen

In this work, we propose a scheme to realize the layer Hall effect in the ferromagnetic topological insulator Bi2Se3 via proximity to d-wave altermagnets. We show that an altermagnet and an in-plane magnetic field applied near one surface gap the corresponding Dirac cone, yielding an altermagnet-ind…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165418] Published Mon Apr 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Layer Hall effect induced by altermagnetism
item.dc:creator: Fang Qin (覃昉) and Rui Chen

item.title: Various electronic crystal phases in rhombohedral graphene multilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Wangqian Miao and Chu Li

We systematically investigate the emergence of electron crystal phases in rhombohedral multilayer graphene using comprehensive self-consistent Hartree Fock calculations combined with ab initio tight binding model. As the carrier density increases, we uncover an isospin cascade sequence of phase tran…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155136] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wangqian Miao and Chu Li

We systematically investigate the emergence of electron crystal phases in rhombohedral multilayer graphene using comprehensive self-consistent Hartree Fock calculations combined with ab initio tight binding model. As the carrier density increases, we uncover an isospin cascade sequence of phase tran…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155136] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

item.title: Laser-induced commensurate-incommensurate transition of charge order in a Hubbard superlattice
item.description:
Author(s): Hua Chai, Zhenyu Cheng, Qinxin Hu, Zhongbing Huang, Xiang Hu, Xuedong Tian, and Liang Du

We investigate the nonequilibrium dynamics of charge density waves in a pumped one-dimensional Hubbard superlattice with staggered on-site Coulomb interactions at half filling, using time-dependent exact diagonalization. In equilibrium, the system exhibits commensurate charge correlations consistent…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165134] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hua Chai, Zhenyu Cheng, Qinxin Hu, Zhongbing Huang, Xiang Hu, Xuedong Tian, and Liang Du

We investigate the nonequilibrium dynamics of charge density waves in a pumped one-dimensional Hubbard superlattice with staggered on-site Coulomb interactions at half filling, using time-dependent exact diagonalization. In equilibrium, the system exhibits commensurate charge correlations consistent…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165134] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

item.title: Hund-projected Kanamori model: An effective description of Hund's metals near the Mott insulating regime
item.description:
Author(s): Johan Carlström

Hund's coupling plays a decisive role in shaping electron correlations of multi-orbital systems, giving rise to a class of materials—Hund's metals—that combine local-moment physics with metallic transport. Here, we derive an effective low-energy description of such a system near the Mott insulating …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165135] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Johan Carlström

Hund's coupling plays a decisive role in shaping electron correlations of multi-orbital systems, giving rise to a class of materials—Hund's metals—that combine local-moment physics with metallic transport. Here, we derive an effective low-energy description of such a system near the Mott insulating …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165135] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Hund-projected Kanamori model: An effective description of Hund's metals near the Mott insulating regime

item.title: Proximity-induced superconductivity and emerging topological phases in altermagnet-based heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Ohidul Alam, Amartya Pal, Paramita Dutta, and Arijit Saha

We present a theoretical framework for investigating superconducting proximity effect in altermagnet (AM)–superconductor (SC) heterostructures. In general, AMs, characterized by vanishing net magnetization but spin-split electronic spectra, provide a promising platform for realizing unconventional q…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155429] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ohidul Alam, Amartya Pal, Paramita Dutta, and Arijit Saha

We present a theoretical framework for investigating superconducting proximity effect in altermagnet (AM)–superconductor (SC) heterostructures. In general, AMs, characterized by vanishing net magnetization but spin-split electronic spectra, provide a promising platform for realizing unconventional q…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155429] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Proximity-induced superconductivity and emerging topological phases in altermagnet-based heterostructures

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Multichannel Kondo effect in one-dimensional superconducting leads
item.description:
Author(s): Pradip Kattel, Abay Zhakenov, and Natan Andrei

The authors uncover here a rich phase diagram when a quantum spin is placed in an interacting superconducting system, with different phases arising due to the competition of the Kondo and superconducting interactions. Particularly surprising is the appearance of multichannel physics in a gapped superconducting phase. They further show that in each phase, the Hilbert space splits into several towers of excitations. By computing the impurity entropy explicitly in each phase, they show that the excitation towers induce interesting nonmonotonic behavior, which is experimentally accessible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165130] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pradip Kattel, Abay Zhakenov, and Natan Andrei

The authors uncover here a rich phase diagram when a quantum spin is placed in an interacting superconducting system, with different phases arising due to the competition of the Kondo and superconducting interactions. Particularly surprising is the appearance of multichannel physics in a gapped superconducting phase. They further show that in each phase, the Hilbert space splits into several towers of excitations. By computing the impurity entropy explicitly in each phase, they show that the excitation towers induce interesting nonmonotonic behavior, which is experimentally accessible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165130] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Multichannel Kondo effect in one-dimensional superconducting leads
item.dc:creator: Pradip Kattel, Abay Zhakenov, and Natan Andrei
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Role of on-site Coulomb energy and negative charge transfer in the Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

Angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy combined with band structure calculations have shown that the layered transition metal dichalcogenide ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$ is a type-II Dirac semimetal. However, conflicting conclusions were reported regarding the role of electron correlations in ${\mathrm{…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165133] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

Angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy combined with band structure calculations have shown that the layered transition metal dichalcogenide NiTe2 is a type-II Dirac semimetal. However, conflicting conclusions were reported regarding the role of electron correlations in NiTe2. We study core level…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165133] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Role of on-site Coulomb energy and negative charge transfer in the Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Spin-spiral instability of the Nagaoka ferromagnet in the crossover between square and triangular lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Darren Pereira and Erich J. Mueller

The hard-core Fermi-Hubbard model displays a massive spin degeneracy at half-filling: superexchange is forbidden in the hard-core limit, so all spin configurations have the same energy. Introducing a single hole, whose kinetic energy depends on the spin state, leads to magnetic ordering. This ordering is lattice dependent. The authors study here how it evolves as one interpolates between a square and triangular lattice, which can be explored using optical lattices. Using perturbative and variational arguments, they identify a phase transition between a ferromagnet and a spin-spiral state.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161114] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Darren Pereira and Erich J. Mueller

The hard-core Fermi-Hubbard model displays a massive spin degeneracy at half-filling: superexchange is forbidden in the hard-core limit, so all spin configurations have the same energy. Introducing a single hole, whose kinetic energy depends on the spin state, leads to magnetic ordering. This ordering is lattice dependent. The authors study here how it evolves as one interpolates between a square and triangular lattice, which can be explored using optical lattices. Using perturbative and variational arguments, they identify a phase transition between a ferromagnet and a spin-spiral state.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161114] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-spiral instability of the Nagaoka ferromagnet in the crossover between square and triangular lattices
item.dc:creator: Darren Pereira and Erich J. Mueller
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Interplay of charge density waves, superconductivity, and band topology in a kagome boron monolayer decorated by dialkaline earth metals
item.description:
Author(s): Hao-lin Song, Meng-hui Wang, Guang-ren Na, Zhong-hua Cui, and Lei Wang

The interplay of collective electronic orders represents a central theme in condensed matter physics, and kagome lattices offer fertile ground for studying their competition and coexistence. Although metal-decorated two-dimensional boron kagome lattices can be stabilized, their quantum phases remain…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134515] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao-lin Song, Meng-hui Wang, Guang-ren Na, Zhong-hua Cui, and Lei Wang

The interplay of collective electronic orders represents a central theme in condensed matter physics, and kagome lattices offer fertile ground for studying their competition and coexistence. Although metal-decorated two-dimensional boron kagome lattices can be stabilized, their quantum phases remain…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134515] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Superconductivity and geometric superfluid weight of a tunable flat band system
item.description:
Author(s): M. A. Mojarro and Sergio E. Ulloa

We study superconductivity and superfluid weight of the two-dimensional $α\text{−}{\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice with on-site asymmetries, hosting an isolated quasiflat band with tunable bandwidth via a parameter $α$. Within a mean-field approximation of the attractive Hubbard model, we obtain the super…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134516] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. A. Mojarro and Sergio E. Ulloa

We study superconductivity and superfluid weight of the two-dimensional α−T3 lattice with on-site asymmetries, hosting an isolated quasiflat band with tunable bandwidth via a parameter α. Within a mean-field approximation of the attractive Hubbard model, we obtain the superconducting order parameter…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134516] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Superconductivity and geometric superfluid weight of a tunable flat band system

item.title: Fluctuation corrections to the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems
item.description:
Author(s): David Riegler, Jannis Seufert, Ronny Thomale, and Peter Wölfle

We determine the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems in the example of the Hubbard model by calculating the contribution of spin and charge fluctuations to the Gutzwiller approximation mean-field result. We employ the slave-boson formulation of Kotliar and Ruckenstein in its spin-rot…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155131] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): David Riegler, Jannis Seufert, Ronny Thomale, and Peter Wölfle

We determine the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems in the example of the Hubbard model by calculating the contribution of spin and charge fluctuations to the Gutzwiller approximation mean-field result. We employ the slave-boson formulation of Kotliar and Ruckenstein in its spin-rot…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155131] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Fluctuation corrections to the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems

item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Chern junctions in moiré-patterned graphene/${\mathrm{PbI}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yan Sun, M. Monteverde, V. Derkach, K. Watanabe, T. Taniguchi, F. Chiodi, H. Bouchiat, and A. D. Chepelianskii

Here, the authors report dissipationless transport at the charge neutrality point, robust fractional conductance plateaux, and quantum interference phenomena in a BN/graphene/PbI2 moiré heterostructure. These effects arise from Chern junctions between domains with distinct Chern numbers, establishing a new device architecture for exploring correlated and topological quantum phases. They show that in this architecture, ballistic channels can survive the transition between integer quantum Hall effect plateaus, something which was not believed to be possible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155425] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yan Sun, M. Monteverde, V. Derkach, K. Watanabe, T. Taniguchi, F. Chiodi, H. Bouchiat, and A. D. Chepelianskii

Here, the authors report dissipationless transport at the charge neutrality point, robust fractional conductance plateaux, and quantum interference phenomena in a BN/graphene/PbI2 moiré heterostructure. These effects arise from Chern junctions between domains with distinct Chern numbers, establishing a new device architecture for exploring correlated and topological quantum phases. They show that in this architecture, ballistic channels can survive the transition between integer quantum Hall effect plateaus, something which was not believed to be possible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155425] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Chern junctions in moiré-patterned graphene/${\mathrm{PbI}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Yan Sun, M. Monteverde, V. Derkach, K. Watanabe, T. Taniguchi, F. Chiodi, H. Bouchiat, and A. D. Chepelianskii

item.title: Emergent quantum valley Hall insulator from electron interactions in transition-metal dichalcogenide heterobilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Palash Saha and Michał Zegrodnik

We explore the emergence of topological phases in moiré ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ bilayer, highlighting the crucial role of spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions at two holes per moiré unit cell $v=2$. Our analysis uncovers robust quantum valley Hall insulating phase and reveal…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165413] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Palash Saha and Michał Zegrodnik

We explore the emergence of topological phases in moiré MoTe2/WSe2 bilayer, highlighting the crucial role of spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions at two holes per moiré unit cell v=2. Our analysis uncovers robust quantum valley Hall insulating phase and reveals that long-range interactions a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165413] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergent quantum valley Hall insulator from electron interactions in transition-metal dichalcogenide heterobilayers
item.dc:creator: Palash Saha and Michał Zegrodnik
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
